topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink bled across the top of my paper, a stark, unforgiving crimson. A C-minus. I stared at it, the fluorescent lights of the library buzzing overhead seeming to amplify the silence in my own head. I picked up my pen, the plastic cool against my fingertips, and simply laid it back down. My shoulders, which had been tight with a familiar knot of anxiety, seemed to slowly, almost imperceptibly, relax. I took a deep breath, the stale air of the study carrel filling my lungs, and decided to just look at the next assignment. It was a small thing, something manageable. I focused on the neat lines of the next prompt, letting the weight of that single poor mark settle, acknowledge it, and then deliberately shift my gaze forward.

[story 2]

"Well, that's not what I was hoping for," I murmured to myself, the sound barely audible above the shuffling of pages around me. The letter felt heavy in my hand, a physical manifestation of disappointment. I folded it once, twice, then placed it deliberately on the edge of my desk, not crumpling it, just setting it down as if it were an inert object. I stood up, stretching my arms high above my head until my spine popped audibly. The stiffness in my neck eased slightly with the movement. I walked over to the window, watching the rain streak down the glass, observing the world outside with a detached curiosity. The feeling in my chest wasn't sharp anymore; it was just… there, a dull, persistent ache that didn't demand immediate attention.

[story 3]

My jaw tightened for a moment, a brief, involuntary clenching of the muscles beneath my skin. The grade was lower than I had anticipated, a significant dip in the curve. I leaned back in my chair, letting the worn vinyl creak beneath me. I didn't immediately reach for my notes or start re-reading the material. Instead, I closed my eyes, letting the sensation of the low mark wash over me. It felt heavy, like a stone resting on my sternum, but I didn't push against it. I allowed the feeling to simply occupy the space, observing its texture without judgment, waiting for the internal pressure to ease its grip.
